$comment: Bench ratchets (plan §15c P14, §19.4). Updated after each main-merge
bench run. The CI workflow reads this file; a regression beyond the
declared tolerance fails the job (and posts a PR comment summarising).
stable_pass_rate    — % of bench rows verdicting stable-pass at
routing_precision   — positive-prompt precision on the 75 routing
dev_iteration_burn  — separate one-time stream (plan §19.2) for the
P0-P15 build-out. Tracked so steady-state CI
cost is not conflated with infra setup.
Baselines are NULL until the first post-P14 main-merge full-bench run
establishes them. The workflow treats NULL baselines as 'first run; do
not gate' and writes the observed value back; subsequent runs gate.

stable_pass_rate.description: min %% of bench rows that stable-pass at determinism=3
stable_pass_rate.alert_action: fail CI; require codex review on regression

routing_precision.description: positive-prompt precision on the 75 routing evals
routing_precision.alert_action: fail CI; routing-positive regression is a hard-gate

cost_per_run.alert_action: warn on PR; fail on main if sustained

dev_iteration_burn.description: P0-P15 one-time bench infra dev burn (plan §19.2 ~$1850)
